definition: Define the system scope or problem boundary
notes: Used broadly across planning diagnostic and operational artifacts

definition: Acquire information about the system or situation
notes: Stable across most empirical and situational artifacts

definition: Organize information into categories or types
notes: Especially important for SWOT Cynefin STRIDE and other categorical artifacts

definition: Assign meaning to observations or context
notes: Handled orientation assumptions and abstraction but sometimes overlapped with Explain
Strongly reused in causal and diagnostic artifacts

definition: Produce options hypotheses ideas or alternatives
notes: Covered ideation hypothesis formation and candidate diagnosis generation

definition: Judge against criteria goals or evidence
notes: Frequently reused but may be compressing comparison and ranking functions

definition: Commit to an option or conclusion
Change the system or initiate an intervention
notes: Covered implementation deployment and prototyping moves

definition: Test whether a claim conclusion or result holds
notes: Stable across empirical and review loops

definition: Extract lessons or update understanding from experience
notes: Useful for review and learning cycles

definition: Reopen a prior judgment when new evidence or conditions appear
notes: Stable in iterative and adaptive artifacts

definition: Transmit reasoning conclusions or information to others
notes: Useful where alignment reporting or command transmission is structurally important

definition: Order issues options or actions by relative importance or urgency
notes: Candidate added during FE-012A internal run from FMEA Scrum and Hoshin Kanri

definition: Contrast alternatives or states against one another to identify relative differences
notes: Candidate added during FE-012A internal run from Decision Matrix and related comparative structures
